data.description:
## Why

The most-advertised property of the `rebar.llm` framework is: *"`import rebar`
stays dependency-free; only **running** an op needs the `[agents]` extra, and the
absence of the extra degrades gracefully."* There is a dedicated contract test for
the **degradation** half — but it runs in **zero** CI lanes, so a regression that
broke the no-extra fallback would ship green. The validator proved this
empirically and found it *understated*: the 4 skip decorators expand to **10
always-skipped test instances**.

## What

Execute `tests/interfaces/store/test_llm_optionality.py`'s degradation tests in a
CI lane where the `[agents]` extra is **absent**, so `_AGENTS` is False and the
`skipif(_AGENTS)` tests actually run.

## Scope

- In scope: `.github/workflows/optionality.yml` (the clean-core-wheel lane is the
  natural home — it already runs with no extras).
- Out of scope: changing the tests themselves, changing `[dev]`'s dependency on
  `[agents]` (that is intentional — it makes the *positive* LLM tests run in the
  main lane).

## Context (citations)

- **The skipped tests:** `tests/interfaces/store/test_llm_optionality.py:87,113,191,250`
  are `@pytest.mark.skipif(_AGENTS, reason="agents extra installed → degradation
  path not exercised")`. `_AGENTS = agents_extra_installed()` at line 52;
  `agents_extra_installed()` (`src/rebar/llm/config.py:330`) returns True iff
  langchain **and langgraph** + langchain-anthropic + langchain-mcp-adapters are
  importable (via `find_spec`, no import). (Earlier draft omitted langgraph.)
- **Why they always skip in the main lane:** `pyproject.toml:93` lists
  `"nava-rebar[agents]"` inside `[dev]`; `.github/workflows/test.yml` installs
  `pip install -e '.[dev]'` in both the `lint` job (≈ line 41) and the `test` job
  (≈ line 100) → `_AGENTS == True` → all 4 (10 parametrized instances) skip.
- **Why the existing clean lane doesn't cover them:**
  `.github/workflows/optionality.yml` has two lanes — an import-linter lane that
  runs only `tests/unit/test_core_optionality.py` + `tests/unit/test_optional.py`
  (≈ line 39), and a clean-core-wheel lane (≈ lines 58-73) that runs a **hand-written
  inline Python import snippet**, *not* pytest on `test_llm_optionality.py`.
- **Empirical proof:** in the dev venv, `CI=true pytest
  tests/interfaces/store/test_llm_optionality.py -rs` → **10 skipped** (reasons
  pinned to lines 87/113/191/250); `env -u CI` with a clean (no-agents) venv runs
  them.

## Implementation plan

1. Open `.github/workflows/optionality.yml` and locate the clean-core-wheel lane
   (no `[agents]` installed). Confirm what it installs (likely just the built
   wheel / `pip install .` with no extras).
2. Add a step to that lane (after the wheel install, in the clean venv) that runs:
   `pytest tests/interfaces/store/test_llm_optionality.py -m "not external" -rs`.
   - **PLAN-REVIEW CORRECTION — do NOT blanket-deselect the other tests.** The
     review confirmed the file *collects* without jsonschema/mcp (all heavy imports
     are function-local; `import rebar`/`rebar.llm` are lazy), and the ~7
     non-degradation tests **pass or cleanly skip** in a no-agents env — they do not
     break. So prefer running the **whole file**. One parametrization
     (`test_interface_import_pulls_no_agents_stack[rebar.mcp_server]`) needs `mcp`
     via `importorskip`, so `pip install mcp` (and `jsonschema`) in the clean lane
     to cover it. If anything genuinely needs agents, deselect ONLY by the specific
     `_AGENTS`-skip node ids — not a broad "~7 tests" cut.
3. Ensure the lane does NOT have `[agents]` on the path (verify `agents_extra_installed()`
   would return False there — no langchain). If the clean wheel lane already
   guarantees this, no change needed beyond the pytest step.
4. Confirm the conftest network guard and collection guards don't exclude
   `tests/interfaces/` in that minimal environment (interfaces tier may need the
   `mcp`/`jsonschema` test deps — install the minimal `[dev]`-minus-`[agents]` set
   if required, or `pip install pytest jsonschema mcp`).
5. Run the lane logic locally in a fresh venv to confirm the 4 degradation tests
   now PASS (not skip): create a venv, `pip install .` (no agents), `pip install
   pytest jsonschema mcp`, then the pytest command — assert 0 skipped for those 4.

## Tradeoffs

- **Benefit:** restores the safety net for the framework's headline optionality
  contract at near-zero cost (a few CI minutes on an already-existing clean lane).
- **Cost/risk:** the minimal environment may lack test-only deps the interfaces
  tier expects (jsonschema/mcp); the plan installs them explicitly. Keep the
  degradation tests deselected from the *main* lane (they correctly skip there) —
  do not try to force them to run with agents present.
- **Alternative considered:** invert the skip (run when agents *present* too) —
  rejected; the degradation path is meaningless with the extra installed.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A CI lane runs `tests/interfaces/store/test_llm_optionality.py`'s
  `skipif(_AGENTS)` degradation tests with `_AGENTS == False` (agents extra absent).
- [ ] That lane reports those tests as PASSED (not skipped) — verified in the CI log.
- [ ] The main `test.yml` lane still skips them (correctly — agents present there).
- [ ] No new always-skip is introduced; `pytest -rs` in the clean lane shows the
  degradation tests executing.
</content>
data.title: Run the LLM-optionality degradation tests in a no-extras CI lane
